w:r.w:t: They slightly increase the risk of having a blood clot in the veins and arteries, especially in the first
side effects not listed in this leaflet. See section 4.
is particularly important to read the symptoms of a blood clot – see Section 2 “Blood clots”).
Before you can begin taking Lucette, your doctor will ask you some questions about your personal health
and, depending upon your personal situation, may also carry out some other tests.

In this leaflet, several situations are described where you should stop using Lucette, or where the reliability of Lucette may be decreased. In such situations you should either not have sex or you should take extra non-hormonal contraceptive precautions, e.g. use a condom or another barrier method.
Lucette alters the monthly changes of body temperature and cervical mucus.
You should not use Lucette if you have any of the conditions listed below. If you do have any of the conditions
form of birth control would be more appropriate.
thrombosis, DVT), your lungs (pulmonary embolus, PE) or other organs;
C deficiency, protein S deficiency, antithrombin-III deficiency, Factor V Leiden or antiphospholipid antibodies;
and may be a first sign of a heart attack) or transient ischaemic attack (TIA – temporary stroke
this medicine (listed in section 6.). This may cause itching, rash or swelling.
containing ombitasvir/paritaprevir/ritonavir, dasabuvir, glecaprevir/pibrentasvir or sofosbuvir/velpatasvir/voxilaprevir (see also in section Other medicines and Lucette).
clot in the leg (i.e. deep vein thrombosis), a blood clot in the lung (i.e.pulmonary embolism), a heart attack or a stroke (see‘Blood clots’ section below).
clot in the leg (i.e. deep vein thrombosis), a blood clot in the lung (i.e.pulmonary embolism), a heart attack or a stroke (see‘Blood clots’ section below).
and your doctor may need to examine you regularly.
and/or difficulty swallowing or hives potentially with difficulty breathing contact a doctor immediately.

(for example, hearing loss, a blood disease called porphyria, yellowing of the skin or eyes (jaundice), itching of the whole body (pruritus), skin rash with blisters during pregnancy (gestational herpes), a nerve disease causing sudden movements of the body (Sydenham’s
or neck known as “pregnancy patches”). If so, avoid direct sunlight or ultraviolet light.
if you have elevated levels of fat in the blood (hypertriglyceridaemia) or a positive family history
of developing pancreatitis (inflammation of the pancreas).
your doctor how soon after delivery you can start taking Lucette.
Using a combined hormonal contraceptive such as Lucette increases your risk of developing a blood clot
very rarely, they may be fatal.
small. HOW TO RECOGNISE A BLOOD CLOT

, talk to a doctor as some of these symptoms such
for a milder condition such as a respiratory tract infection (e.g. a
leg, especially on one side of the body;
urgent medical attention as you may be at risk of another stroke.
of blood clots in the vein (venous thrombosis).
However, these side effects are rare.
Most frequently, they occur in the first year of use of a combined hormonal contraceptive.
The risk of developing a blood clot in a vein is highest during the first year of taking a combined hormonal contraceptive
combined hormonal contraceptive (the same product or a different product) after a break of 4 weeks or more.
not pregnant, about 2 will develop a blood clot in a year.
Out of 10,000 women who are using a combined hormonal contraceptive that contains levonorgestrel,
Out of 10,000 women who are using a combined hormonal contraceptive that contains drospirenone
(see “Factors that increase your risk of a blood clot” below).
hormonal pill/patch/ring and are not pregnant
young age (e.g. below the age of about 50). In this case you could have a hereditary blood clotting
injury or illness, or you have your leg in a cast. The use of Lucette may need to be stopped several weeks before surgery or while
your doctor when you can start using it again.
some of the other factors listed.
Your doctor may decide that Lucette needs to be stopped.
member experiences a thrombosis for no known reason; or you gain a lot of weight, tell your doctor.
a heart attack or a stroke.
advise you to use a different type of contraceptive;
age (less then about 50). In this case you could also have a higher risk of having a heart attack or stroke;
of developing a blood clot may be increased even more.
you are using Lucette, for example
Breast cancer has been observed slightly more often in women using combination pills, but it is not known

detected in women on combination pills because they are examined by their doctor more often. The risk of breast tumours becomes gradually less after stopping the combination hormonal contraceptives. It is important to regularly check your breasts and you should contact your doctor if you feel any lump.
been reported in pill users. Contact your doctor if you have unusually severe abdominal pain.
depressed mood. Depression can be serious and may sometimes lead to suicidal thoughts. If you experience mood changes and depressive symptoms contact your doctor for further medical advice as soon as
(bleeding outside the seven pill-free days). If this bleeding occurs for more than a few months, or if it begins after some months, contact your doctor as they must find out if anything is wrong.
not taken any other medicines, it is highly unlikely that you are pregnant.
your doctor immediately. Only start the next strip if you are sure that you are not pregnant.
use Lucette. They can tell you if you need to take additional contraceptive precautions (for example condoms) and if so, for how long or whether the use of another medicine you need must be changed.

Do not use Lucette if you have Hepatitis C and are taking the medicinal products containing ombitasvir/paritaprevir/ritonavir, dasabuvir, glecaprevir/pibrentasvir or sofosbuvir/velpatasvir/voxilaprevir,
test results (increase in ALT liver enzyme).
effective in preventing pregnancy, or can cause unexpected bleeding. These include:
non-nucleoside reverse transcriptase inhibitors such as ritonavir, nevirapine, efavirenz)
ask your doctor or pharmacist for advice before taking this medicine.
If you are pregnant, do not take Lucette. If you become pregnant while taking Lucette stop taking it immediately
at any time (see also “If you stop taking Lucette”).
pill while you are breast-feeding you should contact your doctor.
an intolerance to some sugars, contact your doctor before taking this medicinal product.
doctor or pharmacist if you are not sure.
the tablets with or without food, but you should take the tablets every day around the same time.
taken. If, for example you start on a Wednesday, take a tablet with “WE” next to it. Follow the direction of the arrow on the strip until all 21 tablets have been taken.
gap week) bleeding should begin. This so-called “withdrawal bleeding” usually starts on the 2nd or 3rd day of the gap week.
start with the following strip, whether your bleeding has stopped or
when you are not taking a tablet.
Begin with Lucette on the first day of your cycle (that is, the first day of your period). If you start Lucette
a condom) for the first 7 days.
You can start Lucette preferably on
the day after the last active tablet (the last tablet containing the active
your previous pill finish (or after the last inactive tablet of your previous pill). When changing from a combined contraceptive vaginal ring or a transdermal patch, follow the advice of your doctor.
its removal, from an injectable when the next injection would be due) but in all of these cases use extra protective measures (for example, a condom) for the first 7 days of tablet-taking Lucette.
28, use a so-called barrier method (for example, a condom) during the first seven days of taking Lucette.
that you are not pregnant or wait until your next period.
vagina. Even girls who have not yet started to menstruate but have accidentally taken this medicine may experience such bleeding.
your doctor or pharmacist for advice.
late taking a tablet, the protection against pregnancy is not reduced.
be reduced. The greater the number of tablets that you
have forgotten, the greater is the risk of becoming pregnant.

tablets at the same time. Continue
tablets at the usual time. The protection against pregnancy
is not reduced, and you do not need to take extra precautions. If you forget more than one tablet use an additional barrier method such as a condom for 7 days.
two tablets at the same time. Continue taking the tablets at the usual time. Instead of having seven pill-free days start the next strip as soon as you have taken the last tablet.
light or menstruation-like bleeding during the second strip.
You can also stop the strip
first tablet-free period, you may be pregnant. Contact your doctor before you start the next strip.

active substances in the pill will not be fully taken up by your body. The situation is almost the same as forgetting a tablet. After vomiting or diarrhoea, take another tablet from a reserve strip as soon as possible.

If you take the tablets according to the instructions, then your period will begin
. If you have to change this day, make the

to change this to a Tuesday (3 days earlier) start a new strip 3 days earlier than usual. If you make the pill-free period very short (for example, 3 days or less) you may not have any bleeding during this time. You may then experience light or menstruation-like bleeding.
your doctor for advice about other reliable methods of birth control.
to become pregnant. You will be able to calculate the expected delivery date more easily.
to your health that you think may be due to Lucette, please talk to your doctor.
Contact a doctor immediately if you experience any of the following symptoms of angioedema: swollen
difficulty breathing (see also section “Warnings and precautions”).
An increased risk of blood clots in your veins (venous thromboembolism (VTE)) or blood clots in your
hormonal contraceptives please see section 2 “What you need to know before you use Lucette”.
or erythema multiforme (characterized by rash with target-shaped reddening or sores)
clots and the symptoms of a blood clot).
not listed in this leaflet. You
